headline: Revenge Porn Criminalization Law Takes Effect With 85 Cases
context: Malaysia first specific revenge porn law effective from 2025 resulted in 85 prosecutions in its first year of enforcement.

cards.big: 85 revenge porn prosecutions in first year of new criminalization law
cards.sub: Law carries maximum 7 years imprisonment and RM100,000 fine

cards.big: Victims filed 480 police reports but only 85 met evidentiary threshold
cards.sub: Digital forensics required to establish distribution chain for prosecution

cards.big: 78% of victims were women aged 18-35 targeted by former partners
cards.sub: Average time from report to charge was 4.5 months

cards.big: Support services for victims include digital content removal assistance
cards.sub: MCMC issued 1,200 takedown orders for intimate images in 2025

cards.big: Revenge porn law tests whether criminal penalties can deter digital intimate image abuse
85 revenge porn cases after criminalisation. Legal deterrence works only with victim-centred enforcement and support.
